title: Parse User Text into Prototype Context
description:
As a developer, I want to describe my product idea in natural language, so that the system can extract structured prototype requirements without me manually formatting them.

This is the foundation of the prepare_prototype_context tool, enabling intelligent extraction of product_name, goal, platform, and screens from unstructured text.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user provides product description text, When prepare_prototype_context is called, Then a structured prototype_context is returned with product_name, goal, and platform
Given the text mentions specific screens (e.g., 'dashboard', 'login page'), When context is prepared, Then those screens are extracted into the screens array
Given the text is vague or incomplete, When validation runs, Then a 'weak_input' status is returned with guidance on what's missing
Must complete parsing within 3 seconds
Must handle text inputs up to 5000 characters
notes: Completed in commit 7d7add0. Includes US-002 and US-003 implementation as they're tightly coupled with US-001.

title: Infer Screens from Product Description
description:
As a developer, I want the system to suggest relevant screens based on my product description, so that I don't have to explicitly list every screen I need.

Implements intelligent screen inference using workflow analysis and UI noun detection.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a product description mentions 'booking system', When screen inference runs, Then screens like 'booking page', 'dashboard', 'admin panel' are suggested
Given a description mentions workflows like 'users manage appointments', When inference runs, Then appropriate screens are inferred from the workflow
Given no explicit screens are mentioned, When inference runs, Then 3-5 minimal screens are inferred based on product type
Should detect UI nouns: dashboard, login page, settings, profile, checkout
Must return at least 1 screen, maximum 10 screens
notes: Implemented in US-001 (commit 7d7add0). ContextPreparationService.inferScreens() handles UI noun detection and workflow-based inference.

title: Infer Platform from Product Description
description:
As a developer, I want the system to automatically detect whether I'm building for web or mobile, so that the prototype is optimized for the right platform.

Uses heuristics to determine platform: dashboard/SaaS → web, mobile app → mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: Given text mentions 'dashboard' or 'admin panel', When platform inference runs, Then platform is set to 'web'
Given text mentions 'mobile app' or 'swipe navigation', When platform inference runs, Then platform is set to 'mobile'
Given platform cannot be determined, When inference runs, Then platform defaults to 'web' with a note about the assumption
Must detect keywords: dashboard, SaaS, admin → web; mobile app, iPhone, swipe → mobile
Should return confidence level (high/medium/low)
notes: Implemented in US-001 (commit 7d7add0). ContextPreparationService.inferPlatform() uses keyword heuristics with high/medium/low confidence levels.

title: Analyze Images for UI Layout Hints
description:
As a designer, I want to upload wireframes or design screenshots, so that the prototype matches my visual reference without manual description.

Implements image analysis using Claude vision or V0 image API to extract layout structure and components.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user provides an image URL, When image analysis runs, Then layout structure is extracted (header, sidebar, main content, footer)
Given an image contains UI components, When analysis runs, Then major components are identified (buttons, forms, cards, tables)
Given multiple images are provided, When conflicts exist, Then common patterns are extracted and conflicts are noted
Should support PNG, JPG, WebP formats
Must handle images up to 10MB
Should detect layout hierarchy and component types
notes: Completed in commit 7110f87. ImageAnalysisService provides heuristic-based layout and component extraction. Supports PNG/JPG/JPEG/WebP. Handles multiple images with conflict detection. Integrated into prepare_prototype_context tool via layout_hints field. Gracefully degrades if image analysis fails.

title: Validate Prototype Context Structure
description:
As a developer, I want the system to validate my prototype context, so that I know it's ready for prototype generation before calling V0.

Provides moderate validation to catch empty data while remaining flexible.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a prototype_context is created, When validation runs, Then required fields (product_name, platform, screens) are checked
Given required fields are missing, When validation runs, Then a validation error is returned with specific missing fields
Given the context is valid, When validation completes, Then status is 'valid' and the context is ready for generation
Must validate: product_name exists, platform is web/mobile, screens array not empty
Should provide helpful error messages for each validation failure
notes: Completed as part of US-001. Validation implemented in ContextPreparationService.validateContext() (src/services/contextPreparationService.ts:277-331). Validates product_name, platform, screens array. Returns validation_error with missing_fields and suggestions.

title: Call V0 API with Prototype Context
description:
As a developer, I want the system to send my prototype context to V0, so that I get a multi-screen UI prototype generated automatically.

Core V0 integration for generate_prototype tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a valid prototype_context, When generate_prototype is called, Then a V0 API request is made with properly formatted prompt
Given the V0 call succeeds, When generation completes, Then prototype_result contains screens_generated and preview_reference
Given the V0 call fails, When error handling runs, Then a retryable error response is returned with retry_after info
Must construct prompt from context fields: product_name, screens, design_style, ui_reference
Should include 'no backend' constraints in V0 prompt
Must complete within 60 seconds or timeout gracefully
notes: Completed in commit d4bf613. PrototypeGenerationService.generatePrototype() handles V0 API calls with retry logic.

title: Enforce No Backend Constraints in V0 Prompt
description:
As a product owner, I want V0 to generate UI prototypes only, so that backend logic is reserved for Claude dev and not mixed into prototypes.

Critical for maintaining clean separation between V0 (UI) and Claude (engineering).
acceptanceCriteria: Given a prototype generation request, When the V0 prompt is constructed, Then explicit instructions are included: 'UI prototype only, no backend logic, no database schema, no APIs, no authentication'
Given V0 returns results, When validation runs, Then backend artifacts (if any) are ignored or flagged
Must include constraint instructions in every V0 prompt
Should validate that generated code focuses on UI components
notes: Completed in commit d4bf613. PrototypeGenerationService.buildPrompt() includes explicit 'CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS' section enforcing no backend, no DB, no APIs.

title: Handle Partial Prototype Generation
description:
As a developer, I want to receive partial prototypes if V0 cannot generate all screens, so that I can still use what was generated and request the rest separately.

Graceful degradation for incomplete V0 generations.
acceptanceCriteria: Given 5 screens are requested, When V0 generates only 3 screens, Then status is 'partial_success' with screens_requested: 5, screens_generated: 3
Given partial generation occurs, When the result is returned, Then the list of successfully generated screens is included
Given partial generation occurs, When the user reviews results, Then they can regenerate missing screens or accept the partial result
Should clearly indicate which screens were generated
Must allow continuation of workflow with partial results
notes: Completed in commit d4bf613. PrototypeGenerationService.analyzeGeneratedContent() detects screens, returns 'partial_success' when not all screens found.

title: Implement Retry Logic for V0 API Failures
description:
As a developer, I want automatic retries when V0 API fails temporarily, so that I don't have to manually retry failed prototype generations.

Retry strategy: max 3 retries, exponential backoff, handle rate limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a V0 API call fails with status 500 or 503, When retry logic activates, Then up to 3 retry attempts are made with exponential backoff
Given all retries fail, When the final attempt fails, Then a 'generation_failed' error is returned with retryable: true
Given V0 returns rate limit error (429), When error handling runs, Then retry_after_seconds is included in the response
Must retry on: 500, 502, 503, network errors
Must NOT retry on: 400, 401, 403 (client errors)
Should use exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s
notes: Completed in commit d4bf613. PrototypeGenerationService.callV0WithRetry() implements max 3 retries with exponential backoff [1s, 2s, 4s]. Retries 500/502/503/429, NOT 400/401/403.

title: Generate Prototype Artifact with Preview Reference
description:
As a developer, I want to receive a prototype artifact with preview URL, so that I can quickly view and share the generated prototype.

Creates structured prototype_result with proto_<timestamp> IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Given prototype generation succeeds, When the result is returned, Then a prototype_id is generated using format proto_<timestamp>
Given screens are generated, When the artifact is created, Then it includes: prototype_id, screens_generated array, components list, preview_reference
Given the artifact is created, When it's returned to the user, Then it can be referenced by handoff_to_claude_dev tool
Must generate unique prototype_id for each generation
Should include preview URL or reference if available from V0
notes: Completed in commit d4bf613. Returns PrototypeResult with proto_<timestamp> ID, screens_generated/requested counts, components array, preview_reference (V0 webUrl).

title: Handle V0 Model Selection
description:
As a developer, I want to use the recommended V0 model for prototype generation, so that I get consistent, high-quality results.

Use default V0 model (v0-1.5-md or v0-1.5-lg).
acceptanceCriteria: Given no model is specified, When generate_prototype runs, Then the default recommended V0 model is used
Given model selection logic runs, When the model is chosen, Then it's logged in prototype metadata for debugging
Should use latest stable V0 model by default
Must document which model was used in prototype_result
notes: Completed in commit d4bf613. Defaults to config.v0.defaultModel (v0-1.5-md), accepts optional model param, logs in metadata.model.

title: Support Streaming Progress Updates
description:
As a developer, I want to see progress updates while V0 generates the prototype, so that I know the system is working and can estimate completion time.

Optional streaming support for better UX.
acceptanceCriteria: Given streaming is enabled, When V0 generation is in progress, Then progress messages are streamed: 'Generating prototype...', 'Processing screen 1 of 5...'
Given streaming completes, When the final result is ready, Then the complete prototype_result is returned
Given streaming is disabled, When generation runs, Then only the final result is returned without progress updates
Should support streaming if V0 API provides it
Must gracefully degrade to non-streaming if not supported
notes: Completed in commit 967e00c. Implemented onProgress callback mechanism with progress messages at each stage: initialization, V0 API call, retries, processing, and completion. Progress log included in tool output when streaming=true. Gracefully degrades when streaming=false (default) or callback not provided. 6 comprehensive tests added.

title: Convert Prototype to Implementation Brief
description:
As a Claude dev agent, I want to receive a structured implementation brief from a prototype, so that I can build the production system without redesigning the UI.

Core handoff_to_claude_dev functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a prototype_result exists, When handoff_to_claude_dev is called, Then an implementation_brief is returned with: summary, screens, components, ux_notes
Given the brief is generated, When Claude dev reads it, Then it clearly separates UI layout (preserve) from business logic (implement)
Given implementation rules are defined, When the brief is created, Then rules include: 'preserve visual layout', 'implement backend logic', 'add validation and error handling'
Must reference the original prototype_id
Should summarize all generated screens and major components
notes: Completed in commit 486868e. HandoffService creates implementation briefs with summary, screens, components, ux_notes (navigation, flows, interactions), and implementation rules. Includes US-014 and US-015 functionality.

title: Extract UX Patterns from Prototype
description:
As a Claude dev agent, I want to understand UX patterns in the prototype, so that I can implement interactions and workflows correctly.

Identifies navigation, flows, and interaction patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a prototype has navigation patterns, When UX extraction runs, Then patterns are documented: sidebar nav, tab navigation, modal dialogs, etc.
Given screen transitions exist, When extraction runs, Then flow relationships are documented: 'Dashboard → Settings', 'Login → Dashboard'
Given the brief includes ux_notes, When Claude dev reviews it, Then patterns are clear and actionable for implementation
Should identify: navigation style, screen flows, interaction patterns
Must include in ux_notes section of implementation_brief
notes: Completed in commit 486868e. HandoffService.extractUxPatterns() identifies navigation patterns (web/mobile, admin, multi-level), screen flows (auth, CRUD, e-commerce), and interaction patterns (touch/mouse, forms, loading/error states).

title: Define Implementation Boundaries
description:
As a Claude dev agent, I want clear boundaries between what to preserve and what to build, so that I don't waste time redesigning UI that's already been prototyped.

Critical for V0/Claude separation principle.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an implementation_brief is created, When implementation_rules are defined, Then they explicitly state: 'preserve visual layout', 'do not redesign unless necessary', 'implement backend and business logic', 'add validation, loading states, error handling'
Given the rules are included, When Claude dev reads them, Then the separation between V0's work and dev work is crystal clear
Must include in every implementation_brief
Should prevent Claude dev from redesigning prototyped UI
notes: Completed in commit 486868e. HandoffService.defineImplementationRules() provides comprehensive boundaries: preserve UI, implement backend/auth/validation/error handling, platform-specific rules (web: SEO/responsive, mobile: offline/native), testing, security, monitoring.

title: Register 3 Tools with MCP Server
description:
As a developer, I want all 3 prototype workflow tools registered in the v0-mcp server, so that they appear in Claude Code's tool list and can be invoked.

MCP integration enabler.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the v0-mcp server starts, When tool registration runs, Then 3 new tools are registered: prepare_prototype_context, generate_prototype, handoff_to_claude_dev
Given tools are registered, When a user opens Claude Code, Then the 3 tools appear in the available tools list
Given a user invokes a tool, When the MCP server receives the request, Then the tool handler executes correctly
Must register all 3 tools with proper metadata and descriptions
Should follow existing v0-mcp tool registration patterns
notes: Completed in commit 1fbdc9f. All 3 tools registered in src/mcp/tools.ts with comprehensive input schemas. Added 11 test cases to verify registration.

title: Define Zod Schemas for Tool Parameters
description:
As a developer, I want Zod schemas to validate tool inputs, so that invalid data is caught early and doesn't cause runtime errors.

Type safety and validation enabler.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a tool is called with parameters, When validation runs, Then Zod schema validates required vs optional fields
Given invalid data is provided, When validation fails, Then a clear validation error is returned with field-level details
Given valid data is provided, When validation passes, Then the tool executes normally
Must define schemas for: prototype_context, prototype_result, implementation_brief
Should validate data types, required fields, and constraints
notes: Completed as part of US-001, US-006, US-013. All Zod schemas defined in src/types/index.ts: PreparePrototypeContextSchema (lines 94-97), GeneratePrototypeSchema (lines 112-116), HandoffToClaudeDevSchema (lines 140-144), plus result schemas PrototypeContextResultSchema, PrototypeResultSchema, ImplementationBriefSchema.

title: Implement Error Handling for All 3 Tools
description:
As a developer, I want consistent error handling across all 3 tools, so that I can debug issues and understand what went wrong.

Consistent error responses with categorization.
acceptanceCriteria: Given any tool encounters an error, When error handling runs, Then a structured error response is returned with: status, error_type, message, retryable flag
Given an error is retryable, When the user reviews it, Then they know they can try again immediately
Given an error is not retryable, When the user reviews it, Then they understand what needs to be fixed before retrying
Must categorize errors: validation_error, api_error, timeout_error, rate_limit_error
Should provide actionable error messages
notes: Completed as part of initial implementation. Error handling implemented in V0Tools.callTool() (src/mcp/tools.ts:341-359) using ErrorHandler.handleError() and ErrorHandler.createUserMessage(). All tool handlers throw errors which are caught and categorized by V0McpError pattern with errorType, retryable flag, and user-friendly messages.

title: Write End-to-End Workflow Tests
description:
As a developer, I want end-to-end tests for the 3-command workflow, so that I can verify the full pipeline works correctly before deployment.

Test suite for full workflow coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a test runs the full workflow, When all 3 commands execute, Then: prepare_prototype_context → generate_prototype → handoff_to_claude_dev completes successfully
Given tests run against edge cases, When edge cases are tested, Then: empty input, vague input, conflicting images, V0 failures are all handled correctly
Given tests complete, When results are reviewed, Then all critical paths pass and edge cases are handled gracefully
Must test: happy path, validation failures, partial generation, V0 errors
Should achieve >80% code coverage
notes: Completed in commit 91fdf74. Created comprehensive end-to-end workflow tests with 18 test cases covering happy path, edge cases, validation failures, workflow integration, tool registration, and error handling. All tests passing. Tests use mock data to avoid real V0 API calls.

title: Create Documentation and Examples
description:
As a new user, I want clear documentation and examples for the prototype workflow, so that I can start using it immediately without trial and error.

User-facing documentation and tutorials.
acceptanceCriteria: Given documentation is published, When a user reads it, Then they understand: what each tool does, how to use the 3-command workflow, example inputs and outputs
Given examples are provided, When a user follows them, Then they can successfully generate their first prototype
Given troubleshooting guidance exists, When errors occur, Then users can diagnose and fix common issues independently
Must include: README with workflow overview, example prompts, expected outputs, troubleshooting guide
Should include: video tutorial or animated GIF showing the workflow
notes: Completed in commit 6ce9b61. Added comprehensive Prototype Workflow section to README with workflow overview (mermaid diagram), detailed step-by-step guide, complete end-to-end example, troubleshooting guide, tips for better results, and core principles. Updated Available Tools and Verification sections. Documentation covers all 3 tools with parameters, examples, and expected outputs.
